From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Tom O'Neill <tom.oneill@quickcart.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Apr 2025 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: QuickCart renewal: upgrade onramp, rate lattice & SLA items

Tom —

Hope you had a good week. Per our conversation last month, attached is a concise summary of the renewal approach we discussed and two options for Hosted API commitments. High level:

- Option A (Conservative): 75M tokens / month committed, 2.8x burst pocket, standard hosted rate bands.
- Option B (Growth): 150M tokens / month committed, 4x burst pocket, lower per-token rate in the higher tier (net ~22% discount vs Option A).

Key operational asks from QuickCart we need to confirm so engineering can provision capacity without disruption:
- Expected 95th p90 latency SLO target for production traffic (you mentioned ~350ms).
- Typical sequence length / median request tokens (helps sizing batching and KV cache).
- Whether a soft throttle or strict rate limit approach is preferred for early ramp.

I’ve looped Karthik for throughput and rollout ops; he can scope the onboard checklist and burst mapping. Attached: QuickCart_Renewal_Term_Summary.pdf and a starter SOW (QC_SOW_2025.xlsx). If you prefer, we can put the Growth option into a 90-day staged onramp (start at 100M, auto-step to 150M after metrics).

Next steps: can we lock in a preferred option and timing this week? If so I’ll send a DocuSign and route to legal for a quick redline pass.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: QuickCart_Renewal_Term_Summary.pdf, QC_SOW_2025.xlsx

From: Tom O'Neill <tom.oneill@quickcart.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Martinez <elena@quickcart.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Apr 2025 11:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QuickCart renewal: upgrade onramp, rate lattice & SLA items

Ben —

Thanks for the clear options. QuickCart is leaning toward Option B (Growth), but a few asks from our side before we sign:

1) We need clarity on the soft throttle behavior. For checkout microservices, any dropped request above a threshold has a direct revenue impact. Can you confirm that during burst events we'll have a stepped degradation (queued or delayed) rather than hard 429s?

2) We run a modal analysis: median request token size is ~230 tokens, heavy tail to 1,200 tokens for batch imports. How will the burst pocket interact with longer sequences?

3) SLOs: we want the 95th P50 latency to be <=400ms for gen endpoints. If that's outside hosted guarantees, are there compensation credits or a path to dedicated capacity?

Elena and I are available Tue/Wed next week to finalize. Also can you confirm if the 4x burst pocket is available immediately on sign, or staged?

- Tom
Tom O'Neill
Head of Product, QuickCart
+1 617-555-0177

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Tom O'Neill <tom.oneill@quickcart.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Apr 2025 13:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QuickCart renewal: upgrade onramp, rate lattice & SLA items

Tom —

Appreciate the questions — quick answers inline and a proposed next-step plan below.

1) Soft throttle behavior: Redwood Hosted uses a prioritized queuing approach when the service is under sustained load; for traffic that exceeds the committed throughput but within the burst pocket we avoid immediate 429s. Short bursts are queued and served; longer sustained overage triggers a soft-throttle that returns a clear Retry-After with a lower smooth-return window. For revenue-critical endpoints we can configure a stricter fallthrough policy (preferential routing to a reserved pool). I'll have Karthik scope the exact routing policy that maps to checkout flows.

2) Long sequences and burst interaction: the burst pocket is token-based; longer sequences consume more of the pocket. For your heavy imports, we recommend batching those into a dedicated backfill window (off-peak) or enabling sequence-aware batching to cap per-request impact. Karthik: can you add a short ops note to the SOW about recommended backfill windows and a guardrail on long-run jobs?

3) SLA questions: Hosted can commit to a 95th percentile latency SLO of 350–400ms for standard gen models at typical sequence lengths (<=300 tokens). If we see persistent 95th >400ms under the Growth commit, we have two remediation paths: (a) temporary burst extension and prioritized capacity for 30 days, or (b) upgrade to a Dedicated pool (we can include an option in the SOW). Marissa — I’ve cc’d you for a quick confirmation on the credit/remediation language we can offer in the renewal.

Immediate next steps:
- Karthik to add an ops appendix to the SOW with burst behavior mapping and backfill guidance (ETA: Mon 4/14).
- Marissa to provide the legal redline window/turnaround (ETA: Tue 4/15).
- I’ll prepare a DocuSign for Option B Growth with a 90-day staged onramp clause (start 100M -> 150M).

If that works I’ll send the DocuSign today and target signing by Wed.

Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Tom O'Neill <tom.oneill@quickcart.com>
Date: Sun, 13 Apr 2025 10:20:00 -0700
Subject: RE: QuickCart renewal: upgrade onramp, rate lattice & SLA items

Tom / Ben / Karthik —

Legal here. I reviewed the term summary and started a light redline to preserve the commercial intent while making sure remediation and termination mechanics are reasonable for both sides. High level notes:

- Recommended change: Add a clear definition of "burst pocket" consumption and the measurement window (we propose a 5-minute rolling window).
- Credits: For any sustained SLA breach (95th >400ms for >72 continuous hours) propose a service credit equal to 5% of that month's invoice, capped at 50% of that single month's charge.
- Onramp staging: Include a clause that the staged increase to 150M will occur only after basic performance checks in the first 30 days; if checks fail, QuickCart can revert to 100M while we remediate.

I attached the redline (redline_quickcart_terms.docx) with tracked changes and inline comments. If the commercial team is comfortable I can push a cleaned DocuSign package after we align on the credit language.

Marissa Cole

To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodai.com>
Date: 2025-04-12T09:17:00-07:00
Subject: Request for additional trial credits and extension

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the trial access. The team is running parallel experiments and we hit our cap earlier than expected. Can you approve an additional 100k tokens and extend the pilot for two more weeks? We have a procurement review next week but need more runtime to finish a model comparison.

I attached a short usage log that shows the burst pattern — hope that helps. Happy to sign an MPA or provide an updated PO if required.

Best,
Samir
Head of ML, HelixAI

Attachments: helixai_credit_log.csv


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodai.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@helixai.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwoodai.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwoodai.com>
Date: 2025-04-12T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for additional trial credits and extension

Samir —

Appreciate the note. Quick context: our promo policy allows one extension per pilot and we typically limit incremental credit grants for new accounts unless there's an approved procurement commitment. Before I approve anything I need: (1) confirmation that this is a single project (not multiple teams), (2) the procurement contact + PO timeline, and (3) the usage log broken down by project owner.

Looping in Lauren for credit-approval checklist and Priyom for fraud pattern review given the burst. If you can provide the procurement contact and confirm it's a single team we can fast-track a modest top-up.

Best,
Ben
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

Signature: Ben Carter | Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference


From: Priyom Das <priyom@redwoodai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodai.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwoodai.com>
Date: 2025-04-12T11:46:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for additional trial credits and extension

Ben / Lauren —

I pulled the HelixAI usage from the last 30 days. Two red flags: (a) three distinct IP clusters are associated with the account in the past week, and (b) there are five distinct API keys under the same org with near-identical project names. That pattern sometimes signals parallel accounts or script-based cycling to consume promo credits.

I recommend holding any unilateral credit increase until we validate identity/procurement. Suggested steps: request procurement PO + org hierarchy, run an account merge check, and ask external contact to confirm project owners. I can run the deeper logs if legal/customer allows.

Priyom
Trust & Safety Analyst

Quoted: \"On 2025-04-12 09:17, Samir Patel wrote: > ...we hit our cap earlier than expected...\"

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwoodai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodai.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwoodai.com>, Samir Patel <samir@helixai.com>
Date: 2025-04-13T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for additional trial credits and extension

Samir —

Thanks for the logfile. For transparency, our standard approval path for extra credits during a trial is: (1) procurement confirmation (PO or written commitment), (2) single-team attestation, and (3) a maximum 30% one-off credit top-up unless an MPA is in place.

Given Priyom's findings, we also need the technical lead to confirm that these requests are for the same project and that the identified API keys are centrally managed. If HelixAI can provide:
- PO or procurement contact and expected PO date
- Name and email of the technical lead responsible for the project
- Answer to whether multiple teams are running on this pilot

We can provision a conditional 50k token top-up while we validate. If procurement signs an MPA within two weeks we'll consider a larger grant.

Regards,
Lauren Bishop
Senior Sales Ops, Redwood Inference

Attachment: helixai_signed_mpa.pdf (placeholder for executed agreements)


From: Samir Patel <samir@helixai.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwoodai.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Jules Moreau <jules.moreau@helixai.com>, procurement@helixai.com
Date: 2025-04-14T15:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for additional trial credits and extension

Lauren / Ben —

Thanks for the clarity and for flagging the risk analysis. Apologies for the confusion — the burst came from two subgroups within our ML org testing separate pipelines; poor internal coordination on our side. Procurement is looping in now and I copied Jules (acct owner) and procurement. Expect a PO by 2025-04-21.

We confirm: this is a single commercial evaluation and we agree to centralize API key management under Jules. For now, a conditional 50k token top-up is acceptable. Please proceed and we'll get the PO over next week.

Best,
Samir

preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodai.com>
To: Anya Stevens <anya@deepmed.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodai.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodai.com>
Date: 2025-04-10T16:12:00-07:00
Subject: Reserved GPU capacity — recap & requested numbers

Hi Anya — thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap and a couple of clarifying asks so we can size the Dedicated reservation and put a rough price on your table.

Call highlights (my notes):
- Workload: commercial medical imaging inference + text extraction for reports. Mix ~75% decode/generation (report text) and 25% embedding/rerank for similarity search.
- Target latency: p90 generation latency <= 400ms for 512-token replies (interactive clinician UI).
- Desired throughput: peak 80 concurrent sessions during morning batch window; average 350M tokens/day across the fleet (your estimate).
- Token profile: avg request length 120 tokens in, 250 tokens returned (370 tokens total per call).
- Existing on-prem footprint: 4x A40s for model research; want dedicated production in cloud.

What we need from you to firm pricing (can be rough):
1) Monthly tokens (please confirm 350M/day -> monthly). If that 350M is daily, confirm days per month in scope.
2) Target budget band for inference (monthly committed spend range) — you mentioned mid-five-figures to low-six-figures; please indicate a hard max if possible.
3) Any fixed concurrency SLA: does 80 concurrent mean minimum guaranteed or expected peak?
4) Preference on GPU family: do you want H100-class performance or would A100-80GB suffice if we recommend a quantized variant?

Next steps: we can produce a dedicated-capacity sketch with GPU count, expected tokens/$1M unit economics, and a 30/60/90 day rollout plan once we have the numbers above. I attached the Q1 usage export you mentioned (please confirm) and a small template for throughput mapping.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodai.com | (415) 555-0192

Attachments: draft-map-throughput.pdf (throughput mapping template), redwood_dedicated_pricing.xlsx (starter pricing template)


From: Anya Stevens <anya@deepmed.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Procurement <procure@deepmed.ai>
Date: 2025-04-11T09:34:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Reserved GPU capacity — recap & requested numbers

Hi Ben — thanks for the summary, that matches our call. Quick answers below.

1) Monthly tokens: the 350M/day is our estimated peak weekday load for product usage. Realistic monthly calc is ~350M * 20 weekdays + 150M * 10 weekend days = 8.0B tokens/month (ballpark). We can confirm after we run a final export.
2) Budget band: procurement is targeting a committed spend in the $30k–$75k/month range for the first 12 months. Upper bound is flexible if we get strong unit economics and predictable latency.
3) Concurrency SLA: the 80 concurrent sessions is our expected peak that should be covered under reserved capacity during our regional morning shift. We can tolerate short bursts beyond that but want predictable p95/p90 behavior inside the 80 concurrent baseline.
4) GPU preference: we would prefer A100-80GB if it keeps costs in-band, but for certain workflows (embedding-heavy batches) we may want an H100 option. Open to a mixed fleet if you recommend it.

I attached the usage export for last quarter; labels are included. Procurement looped in — they will follow up on contract and MSA language.

Thanks,
Anya
Head of Product | DeepMed Analytics
anya@deepmed.ai

Attachment: usage_export_q1.csv

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodai.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodai.com>
Date: 2025-04-11T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Reserved GPU capacity — recap & requested numbers

Forwarding Anya's reply and the usage export. Quick internal notes:
- If 8.0B tokens/month is accurate, that equates to ~8 * $X per 1M tokens target we need to hit to meet DeepMed budget. They want committed spend <= $75k/month ideally.
- Two pricing levers: (A) dedicated A100 pool (lower cost) with quantized model variants; (B) smaller H100 burst pool for embedding-heavy batch runs.
- Sizing rough calc: assume 1 A100-80GB can sustain ~25M tokens/day for mixed decode/embeddings under optimized runtime with prefix caching and 512 token context. That would imply ~320 A100-days/month -> ~11 A100 GPUs reserved to meet peak throughput with buffer (very rough).
- Ask Rishi for firm throughput modeling (tokens/sec, GPU utilization assumptions, p95 latency targets).

Monica
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodai.com


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodai.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodai.com>
Date: 2025-04-12T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Reserved GPU capacity — throughput sketch for DeepMed

Ben / Monica — did a quick back-of-envelope based on their numbers + our runtime assumptions. Highlights:

Assumptions:
- Model: Llama-2-class 70B quantized (production variant) for generation; open embedding model for similarity (quantized).
- Average tokens per request: 370 (from Anya).
- Peak daily tokens: 350M/day (weekdays). Monthly: ~8B tokens.
- Target p90 <= 400ms for 512 tokens -> requires low-latency serving with KV cache and limited batching during interactive windows.

Throughput / GPU estimates:
- A100-80GB (optimized stack): sustained 6.0M tokens/day per GPU (interactive-focused, conservative) if we prioritize p90 latency. Burstable to ~10M/day with aggressive batching (but latency increases).
- H100-80GB (with optimized kernels): sustained 9.5M tokens/day per GPU with better batch throughput.

Sizing options to hit ~8B/month:
A) Latency-priority (mostly A100s): 8,000M tokens/month / (6.0M tokens/GPU/month) ~= 1,333 GPU-month-hours equivalent -> ~28 A100 GPUs reserved (to keep headroom for peaks and maintenance windows).
B) Cost-priority (mixed): reserve 12 A100s + 6 H100s and use routing to send batch embedding jobs to H100s. Equivalent throughput ~(12*6M + 6*9.5M)=72M+57M=129M tokens/day -> monthly ~3.87B, so still short; scale H100s accordingly.

Unit economics (very rough):
- If Redwood charges $Y per GPU-hour equivalent blended into tokens, goal is to present a price near $X per 1M tokens such that DeepMed's $75k/month budget covers needed reserved capacity.
- We should produce two proposals: (1) latency SLO-first with higher committed spend and better p90; (2) blended capacity-focused to lower unit $/1M tokens.

I'll produce a short spreadsheet with tokens-to-GPU mapping and per-1M-token price bands.

Rishi
Sr. Performance Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Procurement <procure@deepmed.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Anya Stevens <anya@deepmed.ai>
Date: 2025-04-14T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Reserved GPU capacity — recap & requested numbers

Ben — Procurement here. Appreciate the detailed notes. From our side:
- Timeline: procurement is targeting a signed commitment by end of May to coincide with next quarter planning.
- Contract ask: 12-month commitment with option for quarterly scaling reviews.
- Pricing request: please provide two committed pricing options with clear $/1M tokens math and the corresponding GPU reserve counts (A100-only and mixed H100/A100). Please include sample monthly invoices showing token tiers and expected overage behavior.

We will review once we have the formal pricing workbook. Also include draft MSA and a standard SOC2 evidence checklist if available.

Thanks,
DeepMed Procurement Team
procure@deepmed.ai

---
End of thread

title: Production outage Apr 10 — compensation & trial extension
preview:
From: Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@cloudwave.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@cloudwave.io>
Date: Fri, 11 Apr 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Production outage Apr 10 — request for compensation and trial extension

Hi Ben,

We experienced a significant outage on Apr 10 between ~02:00–05:00 UTC that caused multiple failed requests and degraded latency across our production traffic. Our SRE team captured logs and a short summary (attached) — the incident impacted two key customer-facing endpoints and triggered automated failovers which still resulted in ~3 hours of elevated error rates.

We signed up for a pilot in March and are in the middle of a proof-of-value; this disruption materially affects our evaluation. Given the customer impact and the shortened evaluation window, we are requesting:

- A goodwill credit to offset the impacted hours (or an equivalent dollar value)
- A 30-day extension of our trial period so we can complete full validation

Can you advise on next steps and whether Redwood can escalate a credit and trial extension? I attached the incident logs (incident-logs-20250410.zip) and a short timeline.

Thanks,

Jamie Lee
Head of Platform
Cloudwave
jamie.lee@cloudwave.io

Attachment: incident-logs-20250410.zip


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@cloudwave.io>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Apr 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production outage Apr 10 — request for compensation and trial extension

Hi Jamie — thanks for the heads up and for sending the logs. I'm really sorry Cloudwave ran into this during the pilot window.

I appreciate the timeline and the impact details. I'm looping in Lauren from Customer Success and Markus from Revenue so we can quickly evaluate an appropriate appeasement (credit) and a trial extension.

Lauren / Markus — can you review the attached timeline and propose an amount/approach we can offer today? If needed I can join a quick call.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0142

> On Apr 11, 2025, at 9:12 AM, Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@cloudwave.io> wrote:
> 
> We experienced a significant outage on Apr 10 between ~02:00–05:00 UTC...


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@cloudwave.io>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Billing <billing@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Apr 2025 08:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production outage Apr 10 — request for compensation and trial extension

Hi all — thanks for the detail, Jamie, and sorry again for the disruption. I reviewed Cloudwave's usage patterns and the incident timeline. Based on impact to production and pilot status, my recommendation is:

- Goodwill credit equal to one month of anticipated committed spend (we estimate ~$5,000 based on current usage)
- 30-day extension of the trial starting from Apr 13, 2025

I've prepared a draft credit note (proposed_credit_note_draft.pdf) for internal review — attaching. If Markus signs off we can route final credit note to billing and send the executed note to Cloudwave.

Proposed next steps:
- Markus: quick approval on the $5,000 credit + 30-day extension?
- Ben: confirm with Jamie that this is acceptable and whether Procurement needs a formal DocuSign or PO.
- Billing: once approved, issue final credit note and update the account.

Thanks,

Lauren Bishop
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com

Attachment: proposed_credit_note_draft.pdf


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Billing <billing@redwoodinference.com>, Legal <legal@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 13 Apr 2025 09:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production outage Apr 10 — request for compensation and trial extension

Approved.

Ben / Lauren — authorize the $5,000 goodwill credit and the 30-day trial extension. Please make the credit note final, copy Legal if Procurement asks for contract language, and run the calibration in HubSpot under deal D-48321. Let Billing know this is an exception under the incident credits policy.

I'll step in if Procurement requests anything beyond the standard credit note.

Markus Klein
VP Revenue | Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwoodinference.com

> On Apr 12, 2025, at 8:20 AM, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> 
> I've prepared a draft credit note (proposed_credit_note_draft.pdf) for internal review...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@cloudwave.io>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Billing <billing@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 13 Apr 2025 15:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production outage Apr 10 — confirmation of credit and trial extension

Hi Jamie — thanks for your patience. Per the attached final note, we've issued a $5,000 goodwill credit to Cloudwave and extended your trial by 30 days (new trial end date: May 13, 2025). The billing team will apply the credit to your account within two billing cycles.

Attachments: credit_note-20250413.pdf
HubSpot record: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/48321
Incident summary (internal): https://redwoodinference.sharepoint.com/incident/20250410-summary

If Procurement needs a signed DocuSign, let me know and we'll route it. Appreciate the partnership and sorry again for the disruption — we want to make sure Cloudwave can finish the evaluation cleanly.

Best,

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0142

> On Apr 13, 2025, at 9:37 AM, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> 
> Approved.

title: Dedicated capacity sizing and soft-hold request for Aurora Labs
preview:
From: Sara Lopez <sara@auroralabs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-04-11T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Dedicated capacity sizing for Aurora Labs — capacity and timing

Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We're entering the procurement window and need to lock down sizing and a soft hold while we finalize commercial terms. High level ask below — please let me know if we should open a formal capacity request ticket.

- Target start: 2025-06-01 (pilot ramp to prod over 6 weeks)
- Desired capacity: equivalent of 12x A100 80GB (sustained for peak periods)
- Latency target: P90 < 120ms for 8k-context inference (chat workload)
- Regional preference: us-west (for lower egress)
- Soft-hold request window: 2025-06-01 through 2025-06-30 (we may convert to reserved capacity if PO finalizes)

I've attached the draft PO and our SLA requirements to this note. If you can confirm availability and a short fallback plan (burst / quantized variant) that would help us move to LOI/DocuSign this week.

Attachments: Aurora_PO_draft.pdf, SLA_requirements.csv

Sara Lopez
Director of Product
Aurora Labs
ops@auroralabs.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>

From: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Fwd: Dedicated capacity sizing for Aurora Labs — capacity and timing

Ben / team — inventory snapshot and recommendation below after a quick check of dedicated and standby pools (us-west):

Inventory (us-west pools):
- Dedicated guaranteed pool: 9 x A100-80GB equivalents available immediately
- Standby pool (warm spare / soft capacity): 6 x A100-80GB equivalents (requires 48–72h staging)
- Planned freeslot: 4 units expected freed by 2025-05-20 (maintenance + reimage)

Recommendations:
1) Soft hold: we can place a soft hold for 12 units for the requested window (2025-06-01 to 2025-06-30). This will reserve 9 guaranteed + 3 standby with an auto-burst policy. Soft hold is non-binding and will expire at end of window unless converted.
2) Conversion to reserved: suggest converting 10 units to reserved (monthly commit) and leave 2 on burst/standby. This balances predictability and cost.
3) Fallback options: quantized model profile Q4 (8-bit) likely reduces memory and allows packing to 14 logical equivalents; P90 latency estimate +20–35ms for Q4 depending on prompt length.

Cost delta (ballpark):
- 12x reserved: +42% vs. 10-reserved baseline
- 10 reserved + 2 burst: +18% (burst priced at on-demand premium)
- 8 reserved + 4 burst: similar to 10-reserved with higher variance in tail latency

Next steps: if Ben confirms, I'll create the formal capacity hold (soft) and attach capacity_snapshot_20250411.csv. To convert to reserved we need deal_id and an initial commit (LOI/DocuSign).

Attachments: capacity_snapshot_20250411.csv

Grace O'Connor

title: Precommit: Pebble Labs traffic / token-profile interview
preview:
From: Danielle Park <danielle@pebble.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@pebble.ai>
Date: Wed, 02 Apr 2025 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Precommit: Traffic & token profile for Redwood Dedicated

Hi Ben / Marissa,

Thanks for running with us on the Dedicated conversation — excited to explore a reserved footprint for Pebble's assistant infrastructure. To keep the precommit sizing focused, I attached a short sample of token counts (24h histogram) and our rough traffic summary. Can you confirm whether the attached CSV is sufficient, and share the minimal set of numbers you'd need to generate a first-pass fleet sketch?

Quick context:
- Product: Pebble conversational assistant (multi-turn), integrated on mobile and web.
- Current production: hosted LLM for prototype, looking to reserve for predictability and regional compliance.

We can also share a HubSpot deal and telemetry export if that’s helpful — Tom is on copy and will follow up with files.

Attachments: pebble_24h_token_histogram.csv (sample token histogram, 1 day), brief_product_notes.pdf (not attached here).

Thanks,
Danielle
Danielle Park
Head of Platform
Pebble Labs



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Danielle Park <danielle@pebble.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 02 Apr 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Precommit: Traffic & token profile for Redwood Dedicated

Danielle — thanks for the sample, this is exactly the right direction. A few clarifying questions so we can produce a concrete sizing sketch:

1) Traffic shape: can you share monthly request volumes (approx) and an estimate of MAUs? If you have hour-level qps for a representative peak day, that helps a lot.
2) Prompt vs response: what’s the typical prompt token count distribution (median / 90th) and average/90th response token lengths?
3) Session behavior: what percent of requests are multi-turn sessions (session affinity needed) vs single-turn generation? Typical session length in turns?
4) Concurrency and SLAs: baseline concurrent sessions and peak concurrent requests (p95/p99). What p95 latency are you targeting (e.g., 500ms / 800ms / 1s)?
5) Quality/cost tradeoffs: are you open to int8/4-bit quantization for cost, or does output fidelity need to be strictly float16/higher? Any hard constraints (e.g., hallucination sensitivity)?
6) Compliance & topology: you mentioned regional compliance — do you need VPC/private control plane (Private) or will Dedicated (hosted but reserved) suffice? Any HIPAA/GDPR specific asks?

If you can drop a quick line with answers (or paste a 24h token histogram + 7-day rolling qps), we'll produce a 1‑page fleet sketch and a recommended GPU-sku mix (ballpark) within 48 hours. I'm attaching a mutual action plan template to speed contracting once sizing locks in.

Cheers,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachments: mutual_action_plan_v1.docx



From: Danielle Park <danielle@pebble.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@pebble.ai>
Date: Thu, 03 Apr 2025 08:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Precommit: Traffic & token profile for Redwood Dedicated

Ben — pasted answers below and attached the 24h histogram CSV Tom exported. Let me know if you need raw logs.

- MAU: ~150k active users (mobile + web).
- Monthly requests: ~3.1M LLM calls today (growth to ~5M in 6 months projected).
- Multi-turn vs single-turn: ~72% multi-turn (avg 4.3 turns), 28% single-turn.
- Prompt tokens: median 18, 90th percentile 48.
- Response tokens: mean 320, 90th percentile 760 (we have some long summary flows).
- Typical day baseline concurrency observed: 40 sustained concurrent sessions; peak concurrent spikes to ~240 during promotions (peak hour qps ~420).
- Latency target: p95 generation latency <= 850ms (user-experience sensitive).
- Cache tolerance: we can accept short prefix caching; session affinity preferred for multi-turn.
- Quantization: open to int8 for non-sensitive prompts; prefer float16 for high-recall flows.
- Compliance: must be able to run in a VPC with customer-managed keys (HIPAA adjacency).

Attachments: pebble_24h_token_histogram.csv (hourly bins), sample_prompt_examples.json (not attached).

Tom will send a short export of hour-level qps for a peak week.

Thanks,
Danielle



From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Danielle Park <danielle@pebble.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 04 Apr 2025 16:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Precommit: Traffic & token profile for Redwood Dedicated

Great data, Danielle — super helpful. Based on what you've shared, a few preliminary recommendations before we produce the fleet sketch:

- KV/prefix caching: given median prompt token lengths are small and responses are large, enabling prefix caching for system prompts and frequent user prefixes will materially reduce per-token GPU work for repeats. We’ll model both with and without caching in the sketch.
- Session affinity: important for multi-turn; plan for sticky routing to keep KV cache locality.
- Occupancy estimate: with mean response of ~320 tokens and your p95 latency 850ms target, we expect occupancy per request to be moderate — we’ll run continuous batching sims.
- Suggested SKUs: start with a mix of A10G/RTX-based nodes for the long-tail and reserve a small H100 tranche for peak parallelism and lower-latency heavy generations. We’ll quantify how many of each in the sketch.
- Quantization profile: propose int8 for conversational short-turn traffic and float16 reserved for long summary generations; we’ll show cost delta.
- Fallback & rollout: we recommend a canary routing policy that sends 5–10% traffic to a lower-cost quantized variant to measure quality regressions before full rollouts.

Next steps: please share (a) the 7-day hour-level qps export Tom mentioned, and (b) a 1‑week sample of prompt/response token pairs if possible. Once we have those, we’ll produce: (1) a 1‑page fleet sketch with SKU counts and estimated token/unit costs, (2) a recommended rollout and fallback policy, and (3) an initial mutual action plan populated with timelines.

Attaching a short walkthrough that explains our sizing assumptions and what we simulate for occupancy/batching.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Principal Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
Attachments: precommit-sizing-walkthrough.pdf



From: Danielle Park <danielle@pebble.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 07 Apr 2025 14:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Precommit: Traffic & token profile for Redwood Dedicated

Marissa / Ben — thanks, that plan sounds good. Tom attached the 7-day qps export and I’ll have product paste a 1-week token-pair sample by EOD tomorrow. We’re also happy to schedule a 60‑minute deep-dive — can you do Wed 10am PT?

Attachments: pebble_7day_qps_peak.csv (attached by Tom).

Talk soon,
Danielle
Danielle Park
Head of Platform, Pebble Labs

title: Org access charter: ArcadiaHealth pilot - API keys & admin invites
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Sofia Martinez <s.martinez@arcadiahealth.com>, Josh Reynolds <josh.reynolds@arcadiahealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 22 Mar 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Org access charter: ArcadiaHealth pilot - API keys & admin invites

Hi Sofia / Josh —

Great to connect yesterday. As discussed, this thread will capture the kickoff items for the ArcadiaHealth pilot (3-week internal evaluation + one small production route). Goal is to have a working environment with seeded API keys and admin users by the end of next week so your engineers can exercise the integration.

Key asks to start implementation planning:
- Please confirm the pilot project name you want us to create (we'll create a Redwood project and seed API keys scoped to it).
- Provide a short list of 2–3 workspace admins (name + email + role) — attached is a CSV template (arcadia-admin-roster.csv).
- Any constraints on API key expiry, IP allowlists, or regional data residency (if applicable).

What we will deliver before handoff to CS:
- One project created in your org with two admin API keys (one primary, one rollback).
- Seeded roles and permission map based on your roster.
- MCP Onboarding Checklist completed and shared (attached).

Proposed timeline:
1) Today: Receive admin roster + pilot project name.
2) Mon-Wed: We seed the project, create keys, and run a smoke test.
3) Thu: Handoff call with implementation owner and your engineers.

I've attached the onboarding checklist and a draft MPA for legal to review. Monica & Ben — once Sofia confirms the roster, can you own key seeding and schedule the smoke-test window?

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: arcadia-admin-roster.csv (text/csv), MCP-Onboarding-Checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet), MPA-draft-Arcadia-20250322.pdf (pdf)

From: Sofia Martinez <s.martinez@arcadiahealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Cc: Josh Reynolds <josh.reynolds@arcadiahealth.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Mar 2025 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Org access charter: ArcadiaHealth pilot - API keys & admin invites

Thanks Aditya — we liked the plan. A couple quick notes:
- Pilot project name: arcadia-pilot-telemetry
- Admins (attached as arcadia-admin-roster.csv): Sofia Martinez (ops), Josh Reynolds (eng lead), plus contractor Alan Chu (integration).
- For keys: we prefer 90-day expiry and IP allowlist for our staging CIDR; final production keys will follow stricter rotation once we move past pilot.

Question: do Redwood API keys support scoped read-only tokens for analytics? Our analytics team will want read-only to embeddings + logs. Also, who should we expect as the SOC2 contact for data handling questions?

I'll loop in legal if they have comments on the MPA.

Sofia

Director, Platform Operations
Arcadia Health

title: Runtime LTS GA — go/no-go criteria and remaining risks
Re: RFI - Resilience and FFIEC controls (First National Bank)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Lisa Hammond <lisa.hammond@fnb.com>, procurement@fnb.com
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 Mar 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: RFI - Resilience and FFIEC controls (First National Bank)

Hi Lisa / Procurement team,

Thanks for sending over the RFI. Attached is our initial response for Section A (controls, uptime targets, and audit artifacts). Highlights:
- We meet FFIEC-aligned resilience expectations; runbooks and failover tests conducted quarterly.
- SOC 2 Type II available; summary attached.
- Network diagram and isolation controls attached.

Two clarifications we expect you may ask for:
1) If you want raw logs or a scoped environment for audit review we can provide a limited read-only access window.
2) For on-prem / private deployments we can supply an architecture pack outlining HSM/KMS integrations.

Attached: FNB_RFI_Response_SectionA.pdf; SOC2-summary.pdf; Network-architecture-diagram.png

Next steps: We can schedule a 30-min follow-up to walk through our resilience evidence and agree on any additional artifacts. Rafael and Naomi are copied to answer technical and compliance Qs.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
(415) 555-0123

---
Attachment stubs: FNB_RFI_Response_SectionA.pdf (PDF, 18 pages); SOC2-summary.pdf (PDF, 3 pages); Network-architecture-diagram.png (PNG, 1 file)

From: Lisa Hammond <lisa.hammond@fnb.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@fnb.com
Date: Wed, 19 Mar 2025 11:08:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: RFI - Resilience and FFIEC controls (First National Bank)

Ben,

Thanks for the quick turn. Initial read looks promising. A few items procurement and I flagged that need more detail before we move to vendor scoring:
- Can you provide the most recent tabletop/resilience test report (redacted) and date of last DR failover?
- Confirm RTO/RPO SLAs for hosted vs. private deployments and any differences by region.
- We need an SSP or equivalent that maps controls to FFIEC domains (operations, vendor mgmt, business continuity).

If possible, share answers in an annotated PDF (so we can track line-level responses). Procurement would like the artifacts by EOD Friday (21 Mar). If you need more time, let me know.

Regards,

Lisa Hammond
Director, Vendor Risk
First National Bank
lisa.hammond@fnb.com

On Wed, 19 Mar 2025 at 09:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks again — appreciate the turnaround.

---
Note: procurement@fnb.com requested the vendor to denote any items that require NDA/TPA before data sharing.

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 Mar 2025 13:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FNB RFI - follow-ups for Lisa

Ben / Rafael,

Saw Lisa's follow-up. Draft plan to respond:
1) Tabletop report: redact customer identifiers and provide executive summary + date (we ran a cross-region tabletop on 2025-01-15; full DR failover was a simulated switchover in 2024-11-09 with <5 minutes downtime observed). Attach redacted incident timeline.
2) RTO/RPO: Hosted API (multi-az): RTO 2 minutes for control-plane failover; RPO near zero for session continuity with KV caching; Dedicated pools: RTO 5-15 minutes depending on failover policy; Private on-prem: depends on customer topology — we provide recommended configs and expected RTOs in the SSP.
3) SSP: I will assemble an FFIEC-mapped SSP extract and annotate the PDF. I will pull the control matrix mapping today and upload to Drive.

I'll prepare an annotated PDF and a short cover memo addressing procurement's NDA question (we can provide redacted artifacts without additional NDA; full raw logs require a signed DPA amendment). Rafael — can you confirm the technical numbers for RTO/RPO and attach the network diagram with callouts?

--
Naomi Feldman
Security & Compliance Lead
Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwood.ai

---
Attachment plan: redacted_tabletop_execsummary.pdf (to be created); SSP_FFIEC_extract.pdf (to be created); link to internal Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cFNB-RFI-response

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Lisa Hammond <lisa.hammond@fnb.com>, procurement@fnb.com
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 Mar 2025 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: RFI - Resilience and FFIEC controls (First National Bank)

Lisa / procurement team,

Per your request, please find the attached annotated RFI PDF and the redacted tabletop executive summary. Summary of the deliverables:
- FNB_RFI_Response_SectionA.pdf (annotated with line-by-line responses to the FFIEC-style questions)
- redacted_tabletop_execsummary.pdf (exec summary of 2025-01-15 tabletop + 2024-11-09 simulated failover)
- SOC2-summary.pdf (already provided)
- Network-architecture-diagram.png (with callouts; Rafael updated to show cross-AZ failover paths)

Key points:
- We performed a coordinated failover exercise on 2024-11-09 with an observed recovery window of under 5 minutes for in-flight sessions using our active-standby control-plane pattern.
- For hosted customers in US regions, our standard SLA is 99.95% monthly with an RTO target of under 5 minutes for control-plane events; Dedicated customers can opt into a 99.99% profile with reserved capacity and cross-AZ replication.
- SSP extract maps Redwood controls to FFIEC domains and includes artifact references (SOC 2, runbooks, test logs). We are willing to grant a limited, read-only audit window for on-site reviewers under an executed NDA/DPA amendment.

Links: Artifact bundle on Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cFNB-RFI-response
DocuSign for DPA amendment (if needed): https://app.docusign.com/prepare/abc123-fnb-rfi

If that looks complete, would you like to schedule a 30-min walkthrough next week? Rafael and Naomi can join for the technical deep dive.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
(415) 555-0123

---

title: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
preview:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 20, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: GA scope confirmation: Route-level Experiments (Console Rollouts) — April release train
Attachments: Route_Experiments_GA_Scope_2025-03-20.pdf

Team — confirming GA scope + timeline for Route-level Experiments (“route A/B testing”) in Console Rollouts.

TL;DR
- Target: GA in April release train (Console 2025.04), with region-by-region enablement behind feature flag for the first 1–2 weeks.
- GA focus: safe operations + trustworthy metrics attribution + clear UX.
- Explicit non-goals for GA: full statistical significance engine, customer-defined custom metrics ingestion, cross-region population rebalancing.

GA scope (in)
1) Control plane + config model
- Experiment CRUD + state machine (draft → running → stopping/stopped), server-side validation.
- Deterministic assignment + eligibility rules per ADR-047.
- Stickiness key precedence finalized (SDK/header/customer_id fallbacks) + warnings when key entropy is low.

2) Data plane routing behavior
- Experiment selection applied at route policy evaluation, before fallback/canary resolution.
- Safe defaults if config unavailable (fail closed to control variant, do not “spray” traffic).

3) Telemetry + attribution
- Canonical attribution schema per ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region; model_id gated/sampled as discussed).
- Guardrails: attribution drop-rate monitoring + alerting.

4) Console
- Create/manage workflow (wizard), list/detail views.
- Results view with basic deltas (latency p50/p95/p99, error rate, throughput, cost/token) and guardrail status.
- Audit/change history view in Console.

5) Safety / compliance / ops
- RBAC enforced for mutations (create/update/start/stop/delete) + “break-glass” admin documented.
- Audit events for all lifecycle actions with required export fields for enterprise.
- Guardrails: soft-stop + notifications as default GA behavior.
- Optional hard-stop remains behind kill switch and disabled by default at GA (we can enable per-account later).

GA non-goals (explicit)
- No full statistical significance engine in-product (we can show deltas and guardrail thresholds only).
- No customer-managed custom metric ingestion (Redwood-defined metrics + Redwood eval signals only).
- No multi-region cross-population rebalancing beyond deterministic bucketing.

Open risks / required resourcing (asks)
- SRE: I need a committed block for alert review + game day + on-call readiness signoff (week of 3/31).
- Telemetry/data: we need attribution drop-rate consistently <0.5% in staging across streaming + retries; and a clear backfill plan for schema changes.
- Metrics cardinality: final SRE/Obs signoff on tagging restrictions before widening beta.

Requested confirmations by EOD tomorrow (Fri 3/21)
- Dylan: confirm eng cut line for April train + any scope cuts needed to hit quality bar.
- Sean: confirm SRE coverage for (1) alerts/runbooks, (2) game day, (3) GA go/no-go.
- Priya: confirm cardinality constraints + any “must haves” before we GA-enable by default.
- Logan: confirm data quality/backfill plan and target numbers for attribution drop + propagation lag.
- Marissa: confirm GA messaging (esp. “no stat-sig”) + beta→GA comms plan.

Thanks — attaching the one-pager we’ll use for exec staff sync so we’re aligned.

Evelyn Hart

Scope we should explicitly de-prioritize to protect schedule
- Anything resembling in-Console statistical significance. We should close the loop on the exploratory branch and ensure no UI hooks imply it.

Team — confirming GA scope + timeline for Route-level Experiments (“route A/B testing”) in Console Rollouts.

TL;DR
- Target: GA in April release train (Console 2025.04), with region-by-region enablement behind feature flag for the first 1–2 weeks.
- GA focus: safe operations + trustworthy metrics attribution + clear UX.
- Explicit non-goals for GA: full statistical significance engine, customer-defined custom metrics ingestion, cross-region population rebalancing.

GA scope (in)
1) Control plane + config model
- Experiment CRUD + state machine (draft → running → stopping/stopped), server-side validation.
- Deterministic assignment + eligibility rules per ADR-047.
- Stickiness key precedence finalized (SDK/header/customer_id fallbacks) + warnings when key entropy is low.

2) Data plane routing behavior
- Experiment selection applied at route policy evaluation, before fallback/canary resolution.
- Safe defaults if config unavailable (fail closed to control variant, do not “spray” traffic).

3) Telemetry + attribution
- Canonical attribution schema per ADR-048 (route_id, experiment_id, variant_id, region; model_id gated/sampled as discussed).
- Guardrails: attribution drop-rate monitoring + alerting.

4) Console
- Create/manage workflow (wizard), list/detail views.
- Results view with basic deltas (latency p50/p95/p99, error rate, throughput, cost/token) and guardrail status.
- Audit/change history view in Console.

5) Safety / compliance / ops
- RBAC enforced for mutations (create/update/start/stop/delete) + “break-glass” admin documented.
- Audit events for all lifecycle actions with required export fields for enterprise.
- Guardrails: soft-stop + notifications as default GA behavior.
- Optional hard-stop remains behind kill switch and disabled by default at GA (we can enable per-account later).

GA non-goals (explicit)
- No full statistical significance engine in-product (we can show deltas and guardrail thresholds only).
- No customer-managed custom metric ingestion (Redwood-defined metrics + Redwood eval signals only).
- No multi-region cross-population rebalancing beyond deterministic bucketing.

Open risks / required resourcing (asks)

- Telemetry/data: we need attribution drop-rate consistently <0.5% in staging across streaming + retries; and a clear backfill plan for schema changes.
- Metrics cardinality: final SRE/Obs signoff on tagging restrictions before widening beta.

Requested confirmations by EOD tomorrow (Fri 3/21)
- Dylan: confirm eng cut line for April train + any scope cuts needed to hit quality bar.
- Sean: confirm SRE coverage for (1) alerts/runbooks, (2) game day, (3) GA go/no-go.
- Priya: confirm cardinality constraints + any “must haves” before we GA-enable by default.
- Logan: confirm data quality/backfill plan and target numbers for attribution drop + propagation lag.
- Marissa: confirm GA messaging (esp. “no stat-sig”) + beta→GA comms plan.

Thanks — attaching the one-pager we’ll use for exec staff sync so we’re aligned.

Evelyn Hart
VP Product, Redwood Inference

title: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Team —

I’m putting a lightweight plan together to roll out Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO enablement to Sales/CS (materials + live trainings). Goal is to avoid “what can I promise?” ambiguity and make quoting consistent.

Proposed deliverables (drafts in Drive folder):
1) Seller cheat sheet (1–2 pages): tier summary, what’s guaranteed, burst vs committed, exclusions, how to position Standard vs Plus vs Premium.
2) Enablement deck (15–20 slides): narrative + examples + objection handling.
3) FAQ (internal): measurement basics, credits/escalations, common edge cases.

Proposed schedule:
- Fri 3/14: final review comments in
- Mon 3/17: lock v1 materials
- Wed 3/19 + Thu 3/20: live enablement sessions (recorded)
- Mon 3/24: “internal GA” of materials (posted + pinned)
- Tue 4/1: external-facing messaging can start being used in active opps (post-launch guardrails)

Asks:
- Lauren: confirm customer-facing tier names + 1–2 sentences of positioning per tier (so Sales uses consistent language).
- Finance (Laura/Rishi): confirm discount guardrails + deal desk triggers for Premium (one clear rule set).
- Neha: confirm leadership sign-off on the timeline + whether we should gate usage until 4/1.

Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1rwi-dedicated-enablement-rollout
Confluence landing page (WIP): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PROD/Dedicated+Tiers+%2B+Throughput+SLO

Attaching current drafts for convenience.

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Tiers_Cheat_Sheet_v3.pdf
- Dedicated_Pricing_and_Tiers_Enablement_Deck_v6.pptx
- Dedicated_Throughput_SLO_FAQ_v2.docx

Thanks,
Ava

—

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Looks good.

Tier names are: Standard / Plus / Premium.

Positioning (2-liners):
- Standard: “Predictable dedicated capacity for steady-state workloads. Best-effort burst with clear guardrails.”
- Plus: “Throughput floor + defined burst policy for spiky workloads. Better observability + faster response.”
- Premium: “Highest throughput floor, highest availability posture, and fastest response. Built for customer-facing / revenue-critical apps.”

One request: cheat sheet should be explicit about what *isn’t* covered (customer-side throttling, unsupported models/versions, intentional overload tests without coordination).

I’ll add comments in the deck by EOD Fri.

Lauren

—

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 11:21 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

+1 on the plan.

For guardrails, please include a simple table on the cheat sheet:
- Standard: discount up to X% (no deal desk)
- Plus: discount up to Y% (deal desk if >Y% or non-standard payment terms)
- Premium: any discount requires deal desk, and “premium shape” commitments need Finance sign-off if they change headroom assumptions

I’m not putting numbers in email — I’ll drop them as comments in the Confluence version so we don’t have them floating around.

Also: please add a reminder that “burst” is billable and not a guarantee of infinite headroom.

Laura

—

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 12:07 PM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Adding one nuance for Premium:
- If customer asks to “lock in” a specific GPU type or region where supply is tight, we need a lead time disclaimer and a separate approval step.

I’ll add language suggestions in the FAQ (section: procurement / lead times / shape availability).

Rishi

—

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Mar 13, 2025 at 8:36 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Timeline works.

Please gate “Premium guarantees in writing” until materials are locked (Mon 3/17) and we’ve aligned on the exact SLO phrasing for order forms.

I’m fine with Sales using the narrative starting 4/1 as you propose, as long as:
1) we use the approved SLO language, and
2) we don’t promise a specific tokens/sec number outside the tier/shape tables.

Neha

—

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Mar 14, 2025 at 3:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Thanks all — incorporated initial feedback.

Updates:
- Cheat sheet now has a clear “What we guarantee” vs “What’s excluded” box.
- Added a “Do not say / Instead say” section for AEs (re: unlimited burst, fixed GPU promises, and tokens/sec guarantees outside of shape guidance).
- Added a short slide on “shape availability + lead times” with the approval callout.

Two open items I need help with to lock v1 Monday:
1) SLO wording: can we use the exact phrase from PM-503 / Confluence, or is there still legal review pending?
2) Credits: do we want to mention “service credits may apply” at all in enablement, or keep it to CS-only guidance?

If you can reply with preference by Monday 10am PT, I can freeze the PDFs for training.

Ava

On Thu, Mar 13, 2025 at 8:36 AM Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Timeline works.
> ...

—

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 17, 2025 at 9:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

1) Use the Confluence language (I just updated the section and linked PM-503). Legal review is in progress but we’re not changing the *meaning* — if legal tweaks exact words, we’ll rev the doc.

2) Credits: keep it high-level for Sales (“Remedies handled via Support/CS per contract; don’t negotiate in email”). Detailed policy stays internal.

Also: please highlight that the measurement is based on Redwood-side metrics (not client-side timers) and that streaming has specific accounting (we’ve fixed prior mismatch, but we should set expectations).

Lauren

—

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 4:42 PM PT
Subject: Re: Sales enablement: Dedicated tiers + throughput SLO rollout (training + materials)

Locked v1 materials.

What’s happening next:
- Posted finalized cheat sheet + deck to Confluence (pinned): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SE/Dedicated+Tier+Cheat+Sheet
- Trainings scheduled:
  - Wed 3/19 10:00–10:50am PT (Sales + SE)
  - Thu 3/20 1:00–1:50pm PT (CS + Support)
  Recordings + Q&A will be linked on the landing page.
- Internal GA: Mon 3/24 (materials are “source of truth” for quoting language)

title: Dedicated capacity demo intake — BrightBridge (priority lead)
Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Priya — quick exec update on “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools” with current milestone status + top risks/mitigations. Lauren/Marissa/Connor looping in for visibility.

Where we are (as of Mar 18)
- Control plane plan model + migrations: DONE (behind FF)
  - State machine: draft → running → held → aborted → completed
  - Promote/abort idempotency + audit hooks in place
- Routing primitives (stage-based split/partition selection): DONE for single-region; multi-region rules in final review
- Telemetry tagging for stage_id: DONE; mitigations for metrics cardinality landed (see hotfix)
- Gate evaluation v1 (policy engine): DONE for latency/error/saturation; “missing data” semantics default fail-closed w/ override
- Console: timeline + evidence endpoints DONE; UI work in progress (design ready, implementation ongoing)

Top risks & mitigations

1) Capacity fragmentation / effective throughput drop during early stages
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- On smaller reservations, a 1% / 10% stage can create a partition that’s too small to absorb burst + streaming concurrency → queue depth spikes, 429s, and noisy gate failures.
- We saw a variant of this in beta-like testing (similar to SUP-28522): canary partition reduced effective capacity and caused unexpected 429s.

Mitigations in flight:
- Minimum stage sizing guardrail: validate plan stages against pool size (replica-based minimum where available; otherwise enforce minimum % and headroom).
- “No starvation” routing rule: if canary partition saturates, allow limited spillover to baseline partition *without* violating rollback semantics (still pins in-flight streams).
- Default stage set for Dedicated pools will be conservative for small pools (e.g., 10/50/100 instead of 1/10/50/100) unless pool size qualifies.
- Explicit pre-flight check in Console: shows projected per-stage capacity and blocks promotion if it would drop below headroom threshold.

2) False gate failures / stuck stages due to telemetry lag or noisy signals
What we’re seeing / why it matters:
- Gate eval depends on stage-tagged metrics; telemetry lag or tag mismatch can make gates “unknown” or fail-closed.
- Bursty traffic creates noisy p99 and error-rate swings over short windows → flapping gates, operator fatigue.

Mitigations in flight:
- Staleness detection: gate evaluation will surface “data stale” vs “failed” to reduce confusion; default action is hold (not auto-abort) when data is missing/stale.
- Minimum sample size + windowing: require N requests and a minimum duration before evaluating pass/fail (avoids 3 requests deciding a promotion).
- Conservative defaults for beta: 15m window for latency/sat (vs 5m) + rate-of-change constraints.
- Manual override path is admin-only with mandatory reason string; always emits audit events.

3) Rollback/abort semantics for streaming requests
Concern:
- Mid-stage abort could break in-flight streams if we hard-reroute. That’s customer-visible.

Mitigation:
- In-flight streams remain pinned to their assigned partition; abort affects new requests only. Console + runbook will call this out explicitly.

Beta readiness
- Feature flagging: scoped by account + pool; avoiding partial-region exposure (Connor coordinating release train)
- Audit logging: create/update/promote/abort/force-promote/hold events all present; export pipeline still needs verification (Connor tracking)
- Runbooks: draft exists; oncall escalation path for gate fail (page vs ticket) being finalized

Proposed beta entrance criteria (next ~2 weeks)
- Multi-region routing behavior signed off (Dedicated: region impairment mid-stage)
- Console UI: stage timeline + gate evidence panel functional end-to-end
- Load test evidence: demonstrate no >1% increase in 429s at 10% stage for pools above min size
- “Missing metrics” game-day: verify hold + manual override + audit trail

Asks / decisions
1) Confirm we’re aligned on default fail-closed for missing gate data (with hold/override), at least for beta.
2) Confirm we will block 1% stage for pools below min size and default to 10/50/100 (product-facing change).

Attachments:
- Dedicated_Staged_Rollouts_Exec_Update_2025-03-18.pdf
- beta-readiness-checklist-staged-rollouts.txt

Thanks,
Ava
—
Ava Chen
Director, Platform Programs
Redwood Inference


On Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Thanks Ava.

A couple quick questions so I can relay cleanly:
1) For the “spillover” rule to prevent starvation — are we comfortable that it won’t confuse customers (“I thought 10% was canary”) and that audit/evidence remains deterministic?
2) On telemetry lag → hold: what’s the auto-timeout? I don’t want pools sitting in held state for days without anyone noticing.
3) Any specific beta design partners already confirmed / readiness level?

-Priya


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 18, 2025 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec update: Dedicated staged rollouts — risks, mitigations, beta readiness (Mar 18)

Answers inline.

1) Spillover rule
- We’re scoping spillover as an SRE safety valve, not a silent behavior change.
- Determinism/auditability:
  - Routing decision remains deterministic given (stage, policy version, partition saturation state).
  - We will emit a stage-level annotation + metric when spillover is active (so gate evidence + audit trails can explain “why baseline absorbed some traffic”).
- Customer messaging:
  - Console will continue to show the configured stage (e.g., 10%) but will surface a warning banner if spillover is triggered (“Canary partition saturated; overflow enabled to protect availability”).
  - For beta, we can keep spillover enabled only for Redwood operators (not customer-configurable).

2) Hold auto-timeout
- Proposal from platform/SRE threads: default hold timeout 60 minutes.
- After timeout:
  - If gate data still missing/stale → stays held but pages the owning oncall (and opens a ticket) rather than auto-promoting.
  - If data recovers → re-evaluate gates and unblock promotion (still requires explicit promote action).
- Connor will add the alert wiring to the beta checklist so we don’t miss it.

3) Beta design partners
- Lauren can confirm names in a separate note (some are sensitive), but status is:
  - 1 large Dedicated customer: eager, has prior incident history with upgrades; wants audit trail + strict gates.
  - 1 mid-market Dedicated customer: primarily cares about avoiding 429s / downtime; willing to run staged upgrades off-peak.
- Both are contingent on Console evidence panel being solid (we have a known historical issue class here: telemetry tag mismatches → empty evidence UI, so we’re baking that into the pre-beta validation).

Re: your earlier relay: we’re aligned to keep missing-data fail-closed + hold + admin override for beta. It’s the only posture that passes enterprise change-management expectations without over-automating.

Ava

title: One-week clock drift between regions — demo scheduling options
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Kovacs <anna.kovacs@eurotech.com>, Pierre Dubois <p.dubois@eurotech.eu>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 5 Mar 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: One-week clock drift between regions — demo scheduling options

Hi Anna / Pierre,

Thanks again for your time on the call earlier. Wed love to run a 45-minute product demo next week focused on latency/region routing and multi-tenant isolation for your EU teams. I wanted to flag one thing up front: the US spring-forward DST change happens a week before the EU clocks change this year, so the usual time conversions are off by an hour for one week. That often trips up global calendar coordination.

To avoid back-and-forth, here are three proposed slots that keep the shift in mind (times shown as local equivalents):

1) Tue Mar 11, 09:00 PT / 17:00 CET / 01:00 JST (next day) — earlier EU evening, late APAC
2) Wed Mar 12, 11:00 PT / 19:00 CET / 03:00 JST (next day) — a little later for EU, still OK for engineering attendence
3) Thu Mar 13, 08:00 PT / 16:00 CET / 00:00 JST — avoids the late-night APAC window if you prefer EU daytime

If none of those work, the attached .ics contains a few alternate blocks and Ive added a concise agenda (demo-agenda.pdf) so you can confirm which topics matter most. If you prefer, you can pick a slot directly on my Calendly: https://calendly.com/redwood/camila-eurotech-demo

Quick notes:
- For the week of the mismatch I recommend we avoid scheduling around 16:00 CET — thats when the one-hour offset is most surprising for people toggling between calendars.
- If you need a different regional representation (APAC product owner), we can run a 30-min technical succession session recorded and shared.

Will wait for your pick; happy to shift based on your teams availability.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0143

Attachments: proposed_time_slots.ics (calendar blocks), demo-agenda.pdf (high-level agenda)

From: Anna Kovacs <anna.kovacs@eurotech.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Cc: Pierre Dubois <p.dubois@eurotech.eu>
Date: Wed, 5 Mar 2025 09:47:00 +0100
Subject: Re: One-week clock drift between regions — demo scheduling options

Hi Camila,

Thanks — really helpful callout about the DST week. Were keen on the demo and Marcus (our infra lead) should join from the EU side. Of the options you listed, Wed Mar 12 at 19:00 CET looks best for us. 

Small ask: Europes clocks dont move until the end of March, so for our calendars it will show as 19:00 CET; sounds like that slot maps to 11:00 PT for you, correct?

Also — can you add a quick note to the invite that the meeting is targeting: 
- multi-region latency examples

1) Tue Mar 11, 09:00 PT / 17:00 CET / 01:00 JST (next day) — earlier EU evening, late APAC
2) Wed Mar 12, 11:00 PT / 19:00 CET / 03:00 JST (next day) — a little later for EU, still OK for engineering attendence

If none of those work, the attached .ics contains a few alternate blocks and Ive added a concise agenda (demo-agenda.pdf) so you can confirm which topics matter most. If you prefer, you can pick a slot directly on my Calendly: https://calendly.com/redwood/camila-eurotech-demo

Quick notes:

Will wait for your pick; happy to shift based on your teams availability.

Best,
