title: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Team — requesting leadership approval today so we can lock Beta scope and start partner onboarding this week.

TL;DR proposal (Beta):
- Opt-in Quantization Profile Library with route-level enablement (Hosted + Dedicated). No default-on.
- 2–3 design partners: Acme AI + Northstar Search (confirmed interest), 1 enterprise candidate pending security review (FalconBank).
- Clear launch gates + rollback criteria; publish docs that emphasize “opt-in + customer-run evals.”

Key call-outs / decisions needed:
1) Scope: route-level toggle only (no tenant-wide defaults in Beta). Console + Config API.
2) Profile selection semantics: require explicit versioned profile ref (no “latest” alias) to avoid silent changes.
3) Launch gates: schema/validator, compatibility guardrails, determinism, eval thresholds, dashboards/alerts, and kill-switch.

Attachments:
- Quantization Profile Library — Beta Plan v0.9 (link).pdf
- Quant Profile Beta — Launch Gates Checklist (v1).xlsx

If we can get a “ship Beta under allowlist + gates below” approval by EOD, I’ll update the plan and move us into execution.

Marissa
Product Lead, Optimize


---

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

+1 on route-level only for Beta.

Edits I need reflected before approval:
- RBAC: Only route admins can change quantization settings. Console must show a confirmation step + “what will change” diff.
- Audit: Every enable/disable + profile ref update generates an audit event with actor, route, old/new ref, and resolved outcome.
- Compatibility guardrails: if incompatible, policy engine must deterministically fall back to baseline (and we must emit a “fallback” metric so we can alert).

On semantics: I strongly agree with no “latest”/auto-updating alias for Beta. If we want a “recommended” alias later, we’ll need explicit customer action + visible changelog.

Priya
Platform Sponsor


---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Adding runtime gates I consider non-negotiable for Beta:

1) Determinism:
- For a given (model_id, model_variant, quant_profile_id) we must apply the same kernels/precision path across hosts, or fail closed to baseline.

2) Safety switches:
- Global kill-switch that forces baseline regardless of route config.
- Fast propagation. Target: <5 min worst-case across Hosted.

3) Observability:
- Per-request log annotation: quant_profile_id and resolution outcome (applied/fallback/disabled).
- Metrics: enabled_rate, fallback_rate, and p95 decode latency deltas.

If these are in the gates checklist (and tracked in the release checklist), I’m comfortable.

Mateo
Serving Runtime Sponsor

From: Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:26 AM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Aligned with the approach.

Applied ML edits for the plan:
- We will only publish profiles that pass the quant regression thresholds on Redwood prompt sets (task-family gates + statistical confidence). Any borderline deltas are “block publication” until recalibrated.
- Beta customers must be told (in docs + Console copy): “Enabling a profile can change outputs; run your own evals. Redwood provides guardrails, not guarantees.”
- Require an explicit rollback plan per partner (who flips the switch, what metrics, what timeframe). We can provide a playbook.

Arjun will own the partner-facing “what we need from you” checklist (prompt set, acceptance criteria, who to page).

Maya
Applied ML Sponsor

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>; Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 1:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

I reviewed the gates checklist attachment. Suggested tightening (so we don’t debate on the day-of):

Launch gates (must-pass):
- Config migration complete in staging + canary, rollback tested.
- Kill-switch tested end-to-end (flip -> policy/routing -> runtime baseline) + runbook updated.
- Dashboards live: enabled routes, fallback_rate, 5xx, p95 latency (prefill + decode if available), cost/token delta.
- Alerts:
  - fallback_rate > 1% sustained (15m) => page + auto-freeze rollout
  - p95 latency regression > 10% on enabled routes (30m) => page
  - quality guardrail signal (eval harness) => block further profile publication

Rollout proposal:
- Week 1: internal perf-canary + 0.1% on one design partner route
- Week 2: 1% -> 10% with explicit signoff

If we can align on these gates today, release engineering is good.

Connor
Release / Rollout Lead

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 2:57 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Ava — summarizing requested edits and asking for final approval.

Edits to incorporate into v1.0 Beta Plan:
1) Scope locked to route-level enablement only (no tenant defaults), Hosted + Dedicated.
2) Profile refs must be explicit and versioned (no “latest” alias in Beta).
3) Platform requirements:
   - RBAC limited to route admins; confirmation modal + visible diff
   - Audit events for all changes incl. actor + old/new + outcome
   - Compatibility checks + deterministic fallback to baseline
4) Runtime requirements:
   - Deterministic selection per (model, variant, profile)
   - Global kill-switch with <5 min propagation target
   - Request logs/metrics tag quant_profile_id + applied/fallback/disabled
5) Applied ML requirements:
   - Only publish profiles that pass regression thresholds
   - Explicit customer messaging: outputs can change; run your evals
   - Partner-by-partner rollback owner + criteria
6) Release gates / rollout:
   - Must-pass gates + dashboards/alerts + runbook (per Connor’s list)
   - 0.1% -> 1% -> 10% staged ramp with explicit signoff
7) Design partners:
   - Acme AI + Northstar Search as initial two; FalconBank optional if security review completes without expanding scope.

If you approve this direction, Marissa will issue Beta Plan v1.0 today and we’ll proceed with partner onboarding + execution.

Aditya

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>; Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>; Dr. Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwoodinference.com>; Arjun Desai <arjun.desai@redwoodinference.com>; Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>; Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>; Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 4:48 PM PST
Subject: Re: Quantization Profile Library — Beta plan approval (scope, design partners, launch gates)

Approved with the edits captured below.

Please make sure the Beta Plan v1.0 is explicit on:
- allowlist-only enablement for Beta (no self-serve without review)
- we do not expand scope to tenant defaults or auto-recommendations during Beta
- “no alias” policy for profiles in Beta (explicit version pin)

Marissa: publish v1.0 and proceed.
Connor/Priya/Mateo/Maya: treat the launch gates as hard gates; no exceptions without bringing it back here.

Ava

   - Deterministic selection per (model, variant, profile)
   - Global kill-switch with <5 min propagation target
   - Request logs/metrics tag quant_profile_id + applied/fallback/disabled
5) Applied ML requirements:
   - Only publish profiles that pass regression thresholds
   - Explicit customer messaging: outputs can change; run your evals
   - Partner-by-partner rollback owner + criteria
6) Release gates / rollout:
   - Must-pass gates + dashboards/alerts + runbook (per Connor’s list)
   - 0.1% -> 1% -> 10% staged ramp with explicit signoff
7) Design partners:
   - Acme AI + Northstar Search as initial two; FalconBank optional if security review completes without expanding scope.

If you approve this direction, Marissa will issue Beta Plan v1.0 today and we’ll proceed with partner onboarding + execution.

Aditya


---

- We'd like Marcus (engineering) to get into sandbox credentials and a brief KV/cache discussion — he's asked for 30 minutes at minimum

Elena Park

Subject: Elloway transition plan: MAP, milestones, and handoff cadence

Hi Daniel —

Thanks again for the call earlier. Attached is a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (Elloway_MAP_v1.xlsx) and a condensed list of milestones with owner suggestions. High-level asks in this note: please confirm the proposed go-live target (Aug 12), the project sponsor for weekly check-ins, and whether procurement needs a separate signature path for our standard MSA.

Summary (proposed):
- Kickoff / Technical scoping: Jun 21 (30–45m) — Owner: Marissa (CS Tech Lead)
- Integration sprint 1 (inbound routing + auth): Jun 24–Jul 12 — Owner: Priyom (Solutions Eng)
- Validation / UAT: Jul 15–Jul 26 — Owner: Naomi (Onboarding)
- Production readiness review & final docs: Aug 5–Aug 9 — Owners: Ben + Naomi
- Go-live: Aug 12 (target)

Risks we flagged: SOC2 evidence for data handling, and a short window for UAT if integrations slip. If you prefer an earlier scoping slot I can push things tighter; alternatively we can move go-live +2 weeks.

Action items for Elloway: confirm sponsor, confirm availability for Jun 21 scoping (we'll propose two times), and confirm whether procurement wants redlines in DocuSign (we can route to your procurement alias).

Attached: Elloway_MAP_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet, milestone sheet + owner tab).

Best,
Ben

title: Phased purge choreography: DPA timelines, subprocessor handoff & transient log exception
preview:
From: Claire Newman <claire.newman@medbridge.health>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: MedBridge Security Team <security@medbridge.health>
Date: Tue, 7 May 2024 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Backup purge windows + DPA question

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our security lead asked for a clear plan for how Redwood will handle: (1) phased backup purges across regions, (2) how deletions are propagated to subprocessors, and (3) whether short-lived log retention exceptions (forensics) can be carved out of our DPA.

Can you share a proposed timeline and a short description of the operational handoff to subprocessors? We need this to feed the procurement SOW and legal review.

Thanks,
Claire Newman
Director of IT Security
MedBridge Health

(Attachment: medbridge-questionnaire-202405.zip)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Newman <claire.newman@medbridge.health>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 7 May 2024 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Backup purge windows + DPA question

Claire —

Appreciate the clear ask. High-level proposal attached and summarized below. I'm copying Kimberly (Legal) and Karthik (Infra) for ops/legal inputs.

Summary (high level):
- Phased purge choreography: 3 waves (archival trim, backup purge, tombstone finalize). Waves are region-aware: NA -> EU -> APAC, with configurable hold windows per account.
- Subprocessor propagation: we notify and coordinate via our subprocessor change feed; for deletion we push a signed deletion manifest + S3 lifecycle triggers to each subprocessor where applicable. Our DPA already requires subprocessors to honor customer deletion requests within the agreed erase window; we'll offer an explicit amendment paragraph to call out the phased purge handoff.
- Log retention exception (forensics): we can allow a transient exception (up to 90 days per incident) but it requires documented justification, legal signoff, and a narrow scope (specific log sets only). This will be surfaced in the DPA addendum.

Deliverables I attached: a one-page choreography PDF, a tentative purge-timeline spreadsheet, and a draft DPA amendment that calls out the subprocessor handoff.

Next steps suggested:
1) MedBridge to confirm acceptable hold windows for each wave (default: 14 days archival hold before purge).
2) Legal to review dpa-amendment-draft.docx and feedback on exception language.
3) Infra (Karthik) to validate S3 lifecycle + manifest approach and estimate runbook time.

I can do a 30-min walkthrough tomorrow if that helps.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
(Attachment: redwood-proposal-phased-purge-v1.pdf; purge-timeline-ms.xlsx; dpa-amendment-draft.docx)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Claire Newman <claire.newman@medbridge.health>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 7 May 2024 12:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Backup purge windows + DPA question

Claire / Ben —

Thanks for the proposal Ben. A couple of legal points for clarity before we finalize text:

1) Subprocessor notifications: our standard is 30 days prior notice for onboarding/offboarding. For deletion propagation, we can require subprocessors to confirm deletion within a 30-day acknowledgement window; operational reality is that some subprocessors will need up to 60 days due to snapshot schedules — we should expose that to customers as a max propagation lag.

2) Cross-border constraint: if MedBridge requires EU data to remain within EU, our amendment needs an explicit clause saying Redwood will only engage subprocessors with EU data residency guarantees or provide for an escrow approach.

3) Forensic log exception: legal will require a documented incident rationale and a named approver (e.g., MedBridge CISO) before we enable a retention extension. We should include a template Incident Forensics Request (IFR) as an appendix to the amendment.

I will iterate on the dpa-amendment-draft.docx and send redlines by EOD.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel — Privacy & Security
Redwood Inference
(Attachment: dpa-amendment-redline-notes.txt)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Claire Newman <claire.newman@medbridge.health>
Date: Wed, 8 May 2024 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Backup purge windows + DPA question

Team —

Operational notes from infra side (short):
- Implementation approach: we plan three-step job sequence per region. Step 1: archive index (mark objects as archived + snapshot). Step 2 (T+14 default): lifecycle purge of backup buckets with object tombstoning. Step 3 (T+30 default): final metadata sweep + delete tombstones.
- Subprocessor handoff: we'll produce a signed deletion manifest (JSON + SHA256) and post to a pre-agreed S3 bucket each subprocessor has access to; subprocessors must return a signed ack file. This gives a cryptographic trail for audits.
- For short-lived log exception: we can toggle per-customer log retention flags for up to 90 days. We'll add an auditor-controlled job that will auto-expire and produce a report of entries retained. No manual step required once approved.
- Runbook estimate: ~4 engineer-days to add the signed-manifest handshake + ack digest to current lifecycle pipeline; ~2 engineer-days to expose per-customer toggles and the auto-expiry report.

I'll prepare a runbook snippet and attach it to the HubSpot record.

Thanks,
Karthik
Principal Infrastructure Engineer
(Attachment: runbook-phased-purge-snippet.md)

From: Claire Newman <claire.newman@medbridge.health>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 May 2024 16:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Backup purge windows + DPA question

Ben/Kimberly/Karthik —

This is very helpful, thank you. A few quick clarifications before we share with procurement:
1) Can you change the default T+14 archival hold to T+21 for NA? We have rolling weekly retention that complicates a 14-day window.
2) Please add the IFR template to the dpa amendment as Kimberly suggested.
3) We will need the signed-manifest handshake noted in the SOW; procurement wants a failure-mode clause (what if a subprocessor does not return an ack within 60 days?).

Marissa (copied) will be our procurement lead on this. Let's aim to finalize text next week — Claire available Tue/Wed morning PST for a walkthrough.

—Claire

(Attachment: procurement-checklist.xlsx)

title: Sustained completion delays & mitigation plan for Novonow
preview:
From: Thomas Li <thomas.li@novonow.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ops Team <ops@novonow.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Apr 2024 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Noticeable spike in completion delays this week

Ben —

We're seeing a sustained set of slower completions across our traffic starting Monday evening. title: Helioscope pilot: dedicated capacity sizing and SLO headroom
preview:
From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done). Quick background: our inference pattern is a mix of short chat completions and a reranking endpoint used by our search service. Typical request payloads are ~40–80 tokens; p95 tends to skew towards longer multi-turn flows.

Traffic profile (weekdays):
- Peak concurrent requests (observed): ~60 QPS on search rerank windows (bursty, 30s peaks).
- Average chat QPS outside peaks: 12–18.
- Average tokens per request: 55.
- 95th percentile tokens per request during peak windows: 140.

We care about p95 tail latency <= 300 ms for search rerank (single-turn) and < 700 ms for chat multi-turn flows. Can you help with a rough tokens/sec estimate and how many GPUs we'd need to reserve for Dedicated to meet SLOs with a reasonable headroom buffer? I attached a small CSV with sampled traffic (helioscope_traffic_samples.csv).

Appreciate a short runbook/plan we can show the engineering stakeholders.

Cheers,
Liam
Director of ML Ops
Helioscope AI

Attachments: helioscope_traffic_samples.csv (CSV, 28 KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 11:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Liam —

Thanks — received the CSV and skimmed the burst patterns. Karthik (our deals/solutions lead) and Ben (ops) are looped to help with concrete headroom math. Quick ask: can you confirm the model family you expect to use in pilot (13B vs 70B equivalent) and whether quantized weights are acceptable? That drives per-GPU throughput.

I'll sketch a short, conservative runbook with three scenarios (chat-focused, search-heavy, mixed). Expect a first-pass estimate in the next 24 hours.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference

---
On Fri, Mar 15, 2024 at 9:12 AM Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai> wrote:
> Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done)...

Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
Date: Sat, 16 Mar 2024 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Team —

I pulled the sample CSV and ran quick throughput math. Assumptions:
- Pilot model: 13B-class quantized (we recommend this for cost/latency tradeoffs unless customer insists on a 70B).
- Measured baseline throughput (our conserved expectation): 600 tokens/sec per A100-80GB equivalent when targeting p95 latency budgets around 250–350 ms for single-turn completions. For multi-turn chat with 140-token requests, expect higher GPU time per request; we model on tokens/sec basis.
- Target SLOs: search rerank p95 <= 300 ms (single-turn), chat p95 <= 700 ms (multi-turn averages).

Calculations (mixed workload):
- Peak burst scenario: 60 concurrent requests in 30s window. Use conservative per-request tokens = 140 (p95). Tokens/sec during peak = 60 * 140 = 8,400 t/s.
- Baseline (non-peak) chat: 18 QPS * 55 tokens = 990 t/s.
- Combined peak + baseline (worst overlap): ~9,390 t/s.

GPU demands:
- At 600 t/s per GPU -> raw GPUs = 9,390 / 600 ≈ 15.65 -> 16 GPUs to cover peak sustained tokens/sec (no headroom).
- Add operational headroom: 25% buffer for short-term surges, cache misses, KV overhead -> 16 * 1.25 = 20 GPUs.
- Warm cache / prefix caching can drop effective GPU usage for chat flows with repeated context; assume 10–20% improvement if caching correctly configured, so effective GPUs might be 16–18 in practice.

SLA/fallback considerations:
- If the customer accepts model quantization and batch fusion, we can push per-GPU to ~750 t/s for short bursts, lowering the required GPUs to ~13 raw -> ~16 with headroom.
- For strict low-latency single-turn rerank, reserve a small pool (2–3 GPUs) pinned to low-latency routing to avoid queueing behind heavy chat batches.
- If they require 70B for quality, per-GPU drops to ~120–200 t/s (depending on optimizations) which would multiply GPU needs by ~3–5x; we should call that out as a non-starter for a small pilot.

Next steps for proposal:
1) Confirm model family and quantization tolerance (ask customer).
2) We produce an appendix with token-distribution curves, conservative headroom, and recommended Dedicated slab purchase: recommend a 20-GPU slab for July pilot with canary-scaled burst (add-on) of +4 GPUs for 90-day window.
3) Provide an ops-runbook: cache warm-up schedule, autoscaling throttle windows, and routing policy for rerank vs chat.

I'll attach a quick workbook (capacity_model_v2.xlsx) with the calculations and a short slide we can send to Helioscope.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx

From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 09:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Thanks Karthik — this is helpful and in-line with our internal napkin math. A few clarifications:
- We are comfortable with quantized 13B for the pilot.
- We cannot exceed $X/month for Dedicated slab (I put the exact number in the HubSpot note).
- Search rerank must stay within 300 ms p95; if that requires routing to a hot pool that's fine.

Can you call out an options set where we prioritize cost vs latency? Specifically:
A) Min-cost option that still meets SLOs most of the time (best-effort tails)
B) Latency-priority option (strict tail guarantees)

If you can share the workbook and a single-slide comparative table we can show engineering and procurement next week that would be ideal.

Liam
Director of ML Ops, Helioscope AI

---
Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Liam / team —

Attaching the workbook and a 1-pager (helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf) that lays out two configuration bundles per your ask: Cost-focused and Latency-focused. Summary (short):

Option A — Cost-focused- Reserved slab: 12 GPUs (13B quantized)- Routing: best-effort single pool, opportunistic prefix caching enabled- Expected p95 search latency: ~280–350 ms (tails may spike during sustained 30s bursts)- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Option B — Latency-priority- Reserved slab: 20 GPUs (13B quantized) with a hotpool of 3 GPUs pinned for rerank routing- Routing: policy routes rerank -> hotpool; chat -> shared slab with aggressive caching- Expected p95 search latency: <= 300 ms with <10% chance of tail violations during extreme bursts- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Operational notes:
- We recommend a 14-day warmup before the pilot to prime caches.
- Add a burst add-on (4 GPUs) available for the first 90 days; billing prorated.

I uploaded the finalized workbook to Drive and updated the HubSpot record with the two options. Link below.

Thanks — happy to schedule procurement sync.

Ben Carter
Solutions Ops | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx, helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2bHelioSizingLink, https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/company/helioscope

B) Latency-priority option (strict tail guarantees)

If you can share the workbook and a single-slide comparative table we can show engineering and procurement next week that would be ideal.

Liam
Director of ML Ops, Helioscope AI

---
Attachments: (referenced) helioscope_traffic_samples.csv

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Mar 2024 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Liam / team —

Attaching the workbook and a 1-pager (helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf) that lays out two configuration bundles per your ask: Cost-focused and Latency-focused. Summary (short):

Option A — Cost-focused- Reserved slab: 12 GPUs (13B quantized)- Routing: best-effort single pool, opportunistic prefix caching enabled- Expected p95 search latency: ~280–350 ms (tails may spike during sustained 30s bursts)- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Option B — Latency-priority- Reserved slab: 20 GPUs (13B quantized) with a hotpool of 3 GPUs pinned for rerank routing- Routing: policy routes rerank -> hotpool; chat -> shared slab with aggressive caching- Expected p95 search latency: <= 300 ms with <10% chance of tail violations during extreme bursts- Monthly cost estimate: (redacted)

Operational notes:
- We recommend a 14-day warmup before the pilot to prime caches.
- Add a burst add-on (4 GPUs) available for the first 90 days; billing prorated.

I uploaded the finalized workbook to Drive and updated the HubSpot record with the two options. Link below.

Thanks — happy to schedule procurement sync.

Ben Carter
Solutions Ops | Redwood Inference

Attachments: capacity_model_v2.xlsx, helioscope_mvp_malpresentation.pdf

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2bHelioSizingLink, https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/company/helioscope
